    * [i] UBERON:0000465 ! material anatomical entity
        * [i] UBERON:0000061 ! anatomical structure
            * [i] **UBERON:8480048 ! calf**


Clearly calf is a GAP. It is tempting to put AS under GAP but there are many ASs that are not GAPs. So we need to go through all ASs and so long as they are not some subcellular outlier put them under GAP.

Aside, some lessons learned:

1. Avoid over-abstracting in the first place and inventing categories not actually used by biologists (looking at you, CARO)
2. Try and make the root nodes in upper ontologies align with root nodes in existing ontologies (we still haven't learned that lesson looking at COB)
3. Trying to combine both systems in one can lead to confusion, especially if done piecemeal (this ticket)

{"body":"E.g.:

 * anatomical entity
     * material anatomical entity
        * gross material anatomical entity
        * cell 
        * subcellular anatomical structure 

Note: there is an elephant in the room we haven't discussed which is immaterial anatomical entities.

FAO @matentzn I recall you saying that the phenotype group do include cell but don't include subcellular under AE. Are you sure you have this right?","comments":[{"id":"MDEyOklzc3VlQ29tbWVudDUyMDczMDY4NA==","author":{"login":"matentzn"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"This decision was made due to the fact that cellular components life in GO, while cells and above life in CL and UBERON. I dont think anyone in our effort is strongly opposed to extending the anatomical patterns conceptually to cover sub-cellular stuff. Our DPs should be flexible enough to re-use special GO relationships where it is [appropriate](https://github.com/obophenotype/upheno/blob/master/src/patterns/dosdp-dev/abnormalCellDifferentiation.yaml), but that does not mean the patterns between AEs and CCs should not be aligned (in fact; they are: increasedNumberOf and similar patterns are identical, only with different filler range). 

The only thing I can think of top of my head that was a bit more "conceptual" was that some groups measured the "concentration" of cellular components rather than counting them (increased amount). This would not make sense for AEs. @dosumis is off email atm, but maybe he can also add his perspective here.
","createdAt":"2019-08-13T07:57:06Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/OBOFoundry/COB/issues/45#issuecomment-520730684","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"MDEyOklzc3VlQ29tbWVudDU1NTQ2NzI4OA==","author":{"login":"matentzn"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I am just realising that for our automated matching, it is a bit hard to distinguish cell (which currently resides under anatomy) and cellular components, because GO asserts a cell to actually BE a cellular component. Are there any news on this issue here? {"body":"Hello, 

 For the BP refactoring, we want to untangle multi-cellular organism processes from cellular processes. 
For the 'developmental process' branch, we propose to move 'cellular developmental process' under 'cellular process' and rename 'developmental process' -> 'multi-cellular organism developmental process' 

@thomaspd 

Thanks, Pascale","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwOsb","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Proposed changes: 

- [ ] move 'GO:0048869 cellular developmental process' to 'is_a 'GO:0009987 cellular process'

- [ ] CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development) 

Review children of developmental process: 
- [ ] 'GO:0048532 anatomical structure arrangement' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048856 anatomical structure development' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048646 anatomical structure formation involved in morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0009653 anatomical structure morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0060033 anatomical structure regression' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0043697 cell dedifferentiation > move under GO:0048869 cellular developmental process
- [ ] GO:0043696 dedifferentiation > obsolete, unnecessary grouping class
- [ ] GO:0031128 developmental induction' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
 - [ ] GO:0021700 developmental maturation' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0048757 'pigment granule maturation'  >  not a developmental process (remove is_a developmental maturation')
- [ ] GO:0061792 secretory granule maturation > not a developmental process/ is_a  GO:0033363 secretory granule organization sufficient (remove LD)
- [ ] GO:0016188 synaptic vesicle maturation  > not a developmental process/ is_a   'vesicle organization' sufficient (remove LD)
- [ ] GO:0060074 'synapse maturation'  not a developmental process is_ GO:0050808 synapse organization sufficient (remove is_a developmental maturation')
- [ ] GO:0048469 cell maturation: move under 'GO:0048869 cellular developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0003006 developmental process involved in reproduction': to be reviewed separately, has many cellular processes
- [ ] GO:0022611 dormancy process: is a developmental process? 
- [ ] GO:0098727 maintenance of cell number: is_a ​​GO:0048871 multicellular organismal-level homeostasis ? 
","createdAt":"2023-06-01T23:41:10Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1572924187","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwPRr","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@pgaudet  
CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development)
IS_A developmental process?
If we are to keep the grouping term developmental process, shouldn't cellular developmental process stay? cellular developmental process has dual IS_A parentage.","createdAt":"2023-06-01T23:44:46Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1572926571","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwUJf","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Perhaps we would keep developmental process, IS_A children include
---cellular developmental process
---multicellular organism-level
---- NTR animal developmental process
----  NTR  plant developmental process
---unicellular organism-level (or merge with cellular)
---cellular component-level","createdAt":"2023-06-02T00:11:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1572946527","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5eHXeG","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Discussion with @thomaspd and @raymond91125 
- cellular component-level should go under 'organelle biogenesis' (or something like this, depending on the term 
- for now we can keep the top level term ' developmental process', and decide later when looking more broadly at the ontology
- specifically: under ' developmental process', we can keep for now: 

 - cellular developmental process
-  ~multicellular organism-level~
-  NTR animal developmental process
-  NTR  plant developmental process
-   unicellular organism-level (or merge with cellular) (maybe we create later, when we see whether we need it?)
-  cellular process
- -  GO:0032989 cellular component morphogenesis","createdAt":"2023-06-06T15:30:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1578989446","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5eXFhk","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"New term proposal

I have a complete picture. This is a planning + commentary request, so I'll deliver a detailed plan as a GitHub comment (no file changes — an upper-level refactor of this blast radius needs human sign-off before any PR).

Key findings I'll build the plan around:
- `UBERON:0000001` (literally Uberon's first ID) **already is** "gross anatomical part", but it's a *sibling* of `anatomical structure` under `material anatomical entity` — even though its own definition starts "Anatomical structure that…"

- `calf` and friends are `is_a anatomical structure` *directly* — the smoking gun the user cited

input.command:
cat > /tmp/gap_plan.md <<'EOF'
## A plan for making GAP (gross anatomical part) actually work

I dug through `uberon-edit.obo`. Here is the state of play, the plan, and — as invited — some field notes from the upper-ontology midden.

### What we actually have right now

GAP is not missing. It has been hiding in plain sight as **`UBERON:0000001`** — the very first ID Uberon ever minted, the cornerstone of the cathedral:

```
[Term]
id: UBERON:0000001
name: gross anatomical part
def: "Anatomical structure that is part of a multicellular organism and is at the gross
      anatomical level, e.g. above the level of a cell. ..." [CARO:0001008]
is_a: UBERON:0000465 ! material anatomical entity
disjoint_from: UBERON:0000468 ! multicellular organism
relationship: present_in_taxon NCBITaxon:2759 ! Eukaryota
```

The current upper skeleton:

```
UBERON:0001062 anatomical entity
  └ UBERON:0000465 material anatomical entity
       ├ UBERON:0000061 anatomical structure   ← ~15,600 terms hang here (incl. `calf`, directly)
       └ UBERON:0000001 gross anatomical part   ← 5 terms hang here
```

Things worth noting before we touch anything:

1. **GAP's text definition begins "Anatomical structure that…"** — i.e. the definition asserts the exact `is_a` edge that the logical axioms forgot to add. GAP is a *sibling* of `anatomical structure`, not a child of it. The ontology equivalent of a folder labelled "this lives in X" that has been filed under Y.
2. **GAP is essentially vestigial.** The complete list of its current subclasses:
   - `UBERON:0000463` organism substance
   - `UBERON:0000476` acellular anatomical structure
   - `UBERON:0005423` developing anatomical structure
   - `UBERON:0036215` anatomical surface region
   - `UBERON:6040005` insect synaptic neuropil
3. **`calf` (`UBERON:8480048`) is `is_a anatomical structure` *directly*.** It is not alone — `placental disc`, `apex of urinary bladder`, the kidney loop-of-Henle limbs, and ~40 others are also bolted straight onto `anatomical structure`, bypassing every grouping class that should sit between them.

So the half-finished job isn't "create GAP". It's "make GAP load-bearing again without hand-editing 15,600 stanzas".

### The one insight that makes this tractable

Uberon is an `is_a` DAG. **You do not need to reclassify thousands of terms.** Everything below the direct children of `anatomical structure` inherits its placement transitively. The work is therefore bounded to:

- the **~42 terms with `is_a: UBERON:0000061` asserted directly**, plus
- the handful of deep terms wrongly soldered straight onto `anatomical structure` (`calf` & co.).

Triage ~50 edges, and 15,600 terms fall into place underneath GAP for free. That is the whole game.

### Proposed plan

**Phase 0 — Fix GAP itself (small, mechanical, reviewable on its own).**
- Re-parent GAP: `is_a: UBERON:0000061 ! anatomical structure` (it is already under `material anatomical entity` via AS, so this only *deepens*, never contradicts). This alone reconciles the definition with the axioms.
- Keep `disjoint_from: UBERON:0000468 ! multicellular organism`.
- Add the logical handle implied by the definition: `relationship: part_of UBERON:0000468 ! multicellular organism`.
- Replace the weak `present_in_taxon Eukaryota` hint with the negative constraints requested on the GO call: `never_in_taxon` Bacteria (`NCBITaxon:2`), Archaea (`NCBITaxon:2157`), and the single-celled eukaryote outliers as needed. (Most of this is *entailed* by `part_of multicellular organism`, but explicit constraints help GO and the phenotype ontologies downstream.)

**Phase 1 — Write down the intended skeleton (there is currently no design doc; that is part of the mess).** Target, aligned with COB#45:

```
material anatomical entity
  └ anatomical structure
      ├ gross anatomical part (GAP)              ← the bulk of Uberon
      ├ subcellular anatomical structure         ← mostly GO CC; Uberon holds very few
      └ cell                                     ← CL, imported
```

**Phase 2 — Re-route the ~50 direct `anatomical structure` children (the only real triage).** For each: gross → re-point to GAP (or to a proper gross parent already under GAP); subcellular/abstract outlier → leave under `anatomical structure` (or under subcellular AS). The deep mis-attached terms (`calf` etc.) additionally need a *correct specific* parent so they stop floating one inch below the root.

**Phase 3 — Automated leak detection + a permanent QC guard.** A SPARQL/ROBOT query for "subclass of `anatomical structure`, not under {GAP, subcellular AS, cell}" finds every residual bypass. Fix the hits, then commit the query as a report/validation check in `src/sparql/` so this never silently regresses again.

**Phase 4 — The genuinely new term in this ticket's title: "animal gross anatomical part".** Create `UBERON:99xxxxx ! metazoan gross anatomical part` (syn: *animal gross anatomical part*), `is_a UBERON:0000001`, taxon constraint `only_in_taxon NCBITaxon:33208 ! Metazoa`. This is the metazoan GAP Uberon promised GO. Plant/fungal GAP siblings can follow if anyone wants them.

**Phase 5 — Propagate taxon constraints & hand off to GO** once the tree is stable.

Phases 0, 1 and 4 are low-risk and could go in one PR. Phase 2 is the consequential upper-level decision and wants explicit maintainer sign-off (it ripples into GO, uPheno, Mondo, the works). Phase 3 makes it durable.

### Field notes from the upper-ontology midden (as requested)

You opened the door, so:

- **`anatomical entity` (`UBERON:0001062`) is a stratigraphic dig.** Its xrefs read like core samples: BFO, CARO, AEO, BIRNLEX, NCIT, UMLS, plus AAO/TAO/ZFA/XAO/WBbt/HAO/TGMA all pointing their respective `:0000000` roots at it. Every upper ontology that ever passed through left a potsherd. None of them agreed on what the layer *was*.
- **GAP is `UBERON:0000001`.** We numbered the foundation stone first, then quietly built the entire 15,600-term edifice on the *plot next door* and let the cornerstone hold up five grouping classes and a lone insect neuropil. There is a metaphor about institutional priorities in there somewhere.
- **The definition-as-sticky-note pattern.** "Anatomical structure that is part of a multicellular organism…" with `is_a: material anatomical entity`. The prose knew the right answer the whole time; the axioms just never read it. This is lesson #3 from your own list (piecemeal combination of two systems) caught red-handed.
- On your lesson #1 (over-abstraction / "looking at you, CARO"): the deeper tax wasn't inventing `material anatomical entity` vs `anatomical structure` — it was inventing them and then *not deciding which one was the workbench*, so curators reasonably hung `calf` off whichever root was nearest. Abstraction without an enforced default is just extra rope.
- On lesson #2 (align upper roots with real ontology roots): the fix here is exactly that — make GAP the place biologists' "body parts" actually live, so the upper layer describes the ontology instead of apologizing for it.

The encouraging part: thanks to `is_a` transitivity this is a ~50-edge job dressed up as a 15,600-term job. The mess is real but it is *shallow* — almost all of it sits in the top two centimetres of the hierarchy.

Happy to open the Phase 0 + Phase 1 + Phase 4 PR (re-parent GAP, taxon constraints, the metazoan GAP NTR, and a design doc) on your nod, and to draft the Phase 3 QC query so the next refactor can't be left half-done.

description: Who cmungall is and how to collaborate with them on Uberon
metadata:

@cmungall is a senior Uberon maintainer and a decision-maker on upper-level
ontology structure (the kind of changes that ripple into GO, uPheno, Mondo, CL).

Collaboration notes:
- Explicitly invites and appreciates candid, wry commentary on ontology design
  tradeoffs and historical mistakes (e.g. asked for wry commentary on the
  "upper ontology mess" in issue #2921). Substantive wit lands well; keep it in
  service of the technical point, not gratuitous.
- Thinks in terms of lessons-learned and design principles, not just the
  immediate fix. Framing a task around root causes and durable guards (QC
  checks that prevent regression) resonates.
- Comfortable with deep ontology mechanics — no need to over-explain basic
  is_a/DAG concepts; do surface the non-obvious leverage points.

See also [[feedback-plan-high-blast-radius]], [[project-gap-refactor]].

input.content:
---
name: feedback-plan-high-blast-radius
description: For upper-ontology / mass-reclassification requests, deliver a plan and offer to implement rather than unilaterally editing
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

For high-blast-radius Uberon changes — upper-level re-parenting, mass
reclassification, anything touching root grouping classes — deliver a concrete
phased plan and *offer* to open the PR, rather than unilaterally making the
edits.

**Why:** Issue #2921 asked to "come up with a plan for making GAP work",
explicitly a planning ask. Such refactors ripple into GO, uPheno, Mondo, CL and
need maintainer sign-off; "measure twice, cut once". Low-risk sub-steps (e.g.
fixing a single term's def/axiom mismatch, adding one NTR, a QC query) can be
bundled and offered as one reviewable PR, but the consequential structural
decision stays with the maintainer.

**How to apply:** When a request is phrased as "plan/help think through/how
should we", or when the change re-parents thousands of terms or alters classes
near the ontology root: respond with the plan as a GitHub comment, separate the
safe mechanical phases from the consequential ones, and ask for the nod before
the big move. Routine single-term NTRs/edits do not need this — proceed and PR
as normal per CLAUDE.md.

See also [[user-cmungall]], [[project-gap-refactor]].

input.content:
---
name: project-gap-refactor
description: GAP (gross anatomical part) refactor in Uberon — issue #2921, making UBERON:0000001 load-bearing
metadata:
  type: project
---

Issue #2921: make "gross anatomical part" (GAP) actually work in Uberon.

Key facts (true as of 2026-05-19, verify before acting):
- GAP already exists as **UBERON:0000001** (Uberon's first ID), def from
  CARO:0001008. It was `is_a material anatomical entity` — a *sibling* of
  `anatomical structure` (UBERON:0000061) — despite its def starting
  "Anatomical structure that…". Only ~5 terms classified under it; ~15,600 hang
  off anatomical structure instead. `calf` (UBERON:8480048) was `is_a
  anatomical structure` directly.
- The tractable insight: it's an is_a DAG, so the job is bounded to re-routing
  the ~42 direct children of anatomical structure (+ a few deep mis-attached
  terms), not editing thousands. Descendants follow transitively.
- The issue title also requests a genuinely new term: "animal/metazoan gross
  anatomical part" (is_a GAP, only_in_taxon Metazoa).

**Why:** Discussed on a GO call, flagged high priority; GO wants a metazoan GAP.
Aligns with COB#45 skeleton (gross AP / subcellular AS / cell under material
anatomical entity). Prior implementation was left half-done.

**How to apply:** A phased plan was posted as a comment on #2921 (Phase 0 fix
GAP axioms+taxon constraints; Phase 1 design doc; Phase 2 re-route ~50 edges
[needs sign-off]; Phase 3 SPARQL QC guard; Phase 4 metazoan GAP NTR). If asked
to proceed, Phases 0/1/4 are the safe bundle; Phase 2 needs maintainer go-ahead.

See also [[feedback-plan-high-blast-radius]], [[user-cmungall]].

content.text:
Done. I posted a phased plan + the requested wry commentary as a comment on issue #2921 — no file changes, since an upper-level refactor of this blast radius needs maintainer sign-off first.

**What the investigation turned up:**
- GAP isn't missing — it's `UBERON:0000001` (Uberon's *first* ID), but it's a **sibling** of `anatomical structure` under `material anatomical entity`, even though its definition literally starts "Anatomical structure that…"
- Only **5** terms sit under GAP; the other ~15,600 hang off `anatomical structure`. `calf` is `is_a anatomical structure` directly — the smoking gun you cited.

**The plan, in one line:** because Uberon is an `is_a` DAG, this is a ~50-edge job (re-route the direct children of `anatomical structure`), not a 15,600-term job — descendants follow transitively. Phased as: (0) fix GAP's axioms + negative taxon constraints, (1) write the missing design doc, (2) re-route the ~50 edges [needs your sign-off], (3) a permanent SPARQL QC guard so it can't be left half-done again, (4) the genuinely-new "metazoan/animal gross anatomical part" NTR from the title.

I offered to open the low-risk Phase 0+1+4 PR on your nod. The wry section delivers the requested commentary on the upper-ontology midden (the definition-as-sticky-note pattern, the foundation stone holding up five grouping classes, etc.).
